methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Karman Industries is a Seed-stage climate and energy company founded by David Tearse and Chiranjeev Kalra, having raised $11.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine private frontier-tech peers in Climate & Energy—including Series A companies such as Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—plus one public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis, using the $11.5M total raised as a proxy baseline, applies a typical 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier, implying a valuation of $29M. Peer-Median analysis across the three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. The disparity between methods reflects stage and company-specific maturity differences; the peer set consists primarily of Series A companies further along in development and revenue trajectory.

Triangulating across both methods produces a valuation range of $24M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $89M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around Karman's near-term capital needs, path to Series A, and relative progress versus the peer cohort at Series A entry. Resolution of these uncertainties—particularly demonstrated unit economics and near-term fundraising momentum—will be critical to narrowing the range at the next funding round.
